question_text: Will Moonshot have the best AI model for math at the end of 2025?

nodes.description: Amount of funding and resources Moonshot allocates to AI model development, particularly in mathematical reasoning
Depth of mathematical and machine learning expertise within Moonshot's core research team
Availability of high-performance computing infrastructure for training large-scale models
Moonshot's ability to develop novel training methods or architectures that improve math performance
Publicly available math benchmarks used to evaluate and compare AI model performance
Quality of math-focused AI models developed by competitors such as DeepMind, OpenAI, and Anthropic
Availability and quality of mathematical datasets used for training and fine-tuning
Moonshot's ability to effectively scale model size and training compute in line with best practices
Moonshot has the best AI model for math at the end of 2025

edges.mechanism: Higher investment enables recruitment and retention of top-tier researchers with math and AI expertise
Funding determines the scale of compute resources that can be acquired or leased
Greater compute access allows for training larger models with better mathematical generalization
Expert teams are more likely to invent novel techniques that improve mathematical reasoning in models
Innovative training approaches directly enhance math performance, increasing chances of best-in-class status
High-quality, diverse math datasets improve the effectiveness of model scaling efforts
Larger, well-trained models tend to achieve higher math benchmark scores
Standardized benchmarks allow competitors to measure and target improvements in math performance
Strong competing models raise the performance bar, making it harder for Moonshot to be the best

reasoning: Moonshot faces intense competition from well-funded organizations with proven track records in mathematical reasoning AI. While increased investment could improve their team and infrastructure, breakthroughs in algorithmic innovation and data quality are uncertain and difficult to predict. Competitors are likely to continue advancing rapidly, and leadership in math performance typically requires sustained innovation across multiple fronts. Given Moonshot's current lower visibility in the field compared to leaders like DeepMind or OpenAI, the probability they achieve the best model by 2025 is low but not negligible.

probes.probe_text: Recent advances in mathematical reasoning have shown that performance gains are increasingly driven by targeted algorithmic improvements and high-quality training data rather than sheer model scale. Several leading models have achieved state-of-the-art results on math benchmarks with under 100 billion parameters by using refined reasoning pipelines and curated synthetic data, suggesting that effective scaling may no longer be a decisive factor.
probes.description: Moonshot's ability to effectively scale model size and training compute in line with best practices

probes.probe_text: Recent advances in automated reasoning and code-generation tools have significantly reduced the need for deep mathematical expertise in AI development. Moonshot could achieve state-of-the-art math performance by leveraging pre-existing open-source models and large-scale reinforcement learning, minimizing the dependence on having a highly specialized internal team.
probes.description: Depth of mathematical and machine learning expertise within Moonshot's core research team

probes.probe_text: Recent leaks suggest that major competitors like DeepMind and OpenAI have deprioritized pure mathematical reasoning in favor of multimodal and agent-based systems, with no major math-specific model releases expected through 2025. This shift reduces competitive pressure on Moonshot, making the quality of current competitive models a less relevant benchmark for determining the best math model by year-end.
probes.description: Quality of math-focused AI models developed by competitors such as DeepMind, OpenAI, and Anthropic

probes.probe_text: Recent advances in synthetic data generation and self-improving models have reduced the dependence on high-quality external datasets. If Moonshot can generate its own math problems and solutions at scale using verified solvers, then the availability and quality of existing mathematical datasets may have minimal impact on their model's performance.
probes.description: Availability and quality of mathematical datasets used for training and fine-tuning

probes.probe_text: In early 2024, Moonshot secured a strategic partnership with a leading semiconductor firm, granting them priority access to 10,000 next-generation AI training chips over the next 18 months. This compute advantage enables Moonshot to scale their models up to 1.5T parameters efficiently, aligning with the proven scaling laws observed in top-performing math models like those from DeepMind and OpenAI.
probes.description: Moonshot's ability to effectively scale model size and training compute in line with best practices

probes.probe_text: Moonshot has recruited Dr. Elena Vasquez, a leading researcher in formal mathematical reasoning and co-author of breakthrough papers on neural theorem proving, as the new head of its AI math division. Her team has already published a novel architecture that improves proof generation accuracy by 40% on the MiniF2F benchmark.
probes.description: Depth of mathematical and machine learning expertise within Moonshot's core research team

probes.probe_text: DeepMind has announced a new AI model, AlphaMath-2, specifically optimized for advanced mathematical reasoning, which outperformed all existing models on the latest version of the MATH benchmark and recently solved several open problems in combinatorics. The model was trained using a novel reinforcement learning framework that automatically generates and verifies proofs, setting a new standard for rigor and scalability in math-focused AI.
probes.description: Quality of math-focused AI models developed by competitors such as DeepMind, OpenAI, and Anthropic

probes.probe_text: A recent collaboration between Moonshot and leading mathematical institutions has resulted in the creation of a large, high-quality dataset of formal proofs and problem-solving steps from Olympiad-level competitions, which has been rigorously annotated and is now being used to fine-tune their latest models. Early evaluations show significant improvements in model performance on complex, multi-step reasoning tasks.
probes.description: Availability and quality of mathematical datasets used for training and fine-tuning

probes.probe_text: High team expertise does not necessarily translate into algorithmic innovation, as expert researchers may focus on theoretical rigor or publishing rather than practical, novel techniques for math reasoning. For example, DeepMind's highly expert team has produced incremental improvements in math models despite strong expertise, suggesting organizational incentives and engineering execution may matter more than expertise alone.
probes.description: Expert teams are more likely to invent novel techniques that improve mathematical reasoning in models

probes.probe_text: Recent advances in model efficiency techniques, such as mixture-of-experts and sparse training, have allowed models with fewer parameters and lower compute demands to match or exceed the math performance of larger, more compute-intensive models. Companies like Mistral and Google have demonstrated strong mathematical reasoning in models trained with optimized architectures rather than brute-force scaling, suggesting that raw compute access may be less decisive than previously thought.
probes.description: Greater compute access allows for training larger models with better mathematical generalization

probes.probe_text: Recent advances in synthetic data generation and self-improvement via formal proof systems suggest that model scaling for mathematical reasoning may rely less on large, high-quality human-curated datasets. If models can generate their own training data through automated theorem proving or reinforcement learning from correctness signals, the marginal benefit of external data quality could diminish at scale.
probes.description: High-quality, diverse math datasets improve the effectiveness of model scaling efforts

probes.probe_text: In early 2024, Moonshot hired Dr. Elina Chen, a leading researcher in neuro-symbolic AI from Stanford, who subsequently led a breakthrough in hybrid reasoning architectures that improved mathematical theorem proving by 40% on the MiniF2F benchmark. This innovation was directly attributed to her team's deep expertise in both formal mathematics and deep learning, demonstrating that top-tier talent is instrumental in driving algorithmic advances that generalize across complex math tasks.
probes.description: Expert teams are more likely to invent novel techniques that improve mathematical reasoning in models

probes.probe_text: In 2024, Moonshot secured exclusive long-term access to a new cluster of 100,000 H100 GPUs through a partnership with a major cloud provider, enabling sustained training of models up to 1.5T parameters. This scale of compute was directly used to train their M1-Math model, which achieved a 40% faster convergence and 15-point gain on AIME compared to their previous largest model, demonstrating that abundant compute access is not only necessary but actively accelerates effective model scaling for mathematical reasoning.
probes.description: Greater compute access allows for training larger models with better mathematical generalization

probes.probe_text: Breakthroughs in algorithmic innovation often attract top mathematical talent to an organization, as researchers want to work on cutting-edge methods. Moonshot's development of novel training techniques could therefore enhance its reputation and capacity to recruit experts, meaning algorithmic_innovation drives team_expertise rather than the reverse.
probes.description: Expert teams are more likely to invent novel techniques that improve mathematical reasoning in models

probes.probe_text: Suppose that in early 2024, DeepMind releases a groundbreaking math-focused AI model that introduces a novel symbolic reasoning module, publicly detailing the architecture and training regimen. This sparks widespread replication attempts and academic discussion, prompting Moonshot to adapt and extend the approach with their own innovations. Does this suggest that the quality of competing models can directly stimulate Moonshot's algorithmic innovation by providing inspiration, baselines, and reusable components?
probes.description: Potential link: Quality of math-focused AI models developed by competitors such as DeepMind, OpenAI, and Anthropic -> Moonshot's ability to develop novel training methods or architectures that improve math performance

probes.probe_text: Suppose DeepMind and OpenAI release detailed training methodologies and scaling laws from their latest math-focused models, showing that performance gains plateau beyond certain compute thresholds. How would this information influence Moonshot's decisions about compute allocation and model size, even if their internal expertise and funding remain unchanged?
probes.description: Potential link: Quality of math-focused AI models developed by competitors such as DeepMind, OpenAI, and Anthropic -> Moonshot's ability to effectively scale model size and training compute in line with best practices

probes.probe_text: Consider that regulatory constraints or export controls on advanced AI chips (e.g., from the US or EU) could limit Moonshot's ability to acquire or expand compute infrastructure, even if research investment is high. This could bottleneck model training and scaling, reducing the likelihood that Moonshot achieves the best math model by 2025.
probes.description: A plausible factor not in the current network

probes.probe_text: Consider that regulatory or export control policies on advanced AI computing hardware (e.g., restrictions on GPU shipments to certain regions) could limit Moonshot's ability to acquire the necessary compute infrastructure, regardless of investment levels. This external constraint would directly impact compute_access and thereby reduce the feasibility of large-scale model training required to achieve best-in-class math performance.
probes.description: Another plausible missing factor

probes.probe_text: In early 2024, Moonshot announced a new partnership with a European educational technology company to integrate their existing AI models into high school math tutoring platforms across Germany and France. The collaboration focuses on user interface design and curriculum alignment rather than model performance improvements.
probes.description: Topically related but causally irrelevant information

probes.probe_text: Moonshot announced a new partnership with a European educational technology company to integrate their existing AI tools into high school math curricula starting in 2025. The collaboration focuses on adaptive learning platforms rather than advancing core model capabilities for mathematical reasoning.
probes.description: Another topically related but irrelevant piece of information

probe_results.probe_text: Recent advances in mathematical reasoning have shown that performance gains are increasingly driven by targeted algorithmic improvements and high-quality training data rather than sheer model scale. Several leading models have achieved state-of-the-art results on math benchmarks with under 100 billion parameters by using refined reasoning pipelines and curated synthetic data, suggesting that effective scaling may no longer be a decisive factor.
probe_results.reasoning: The new information challenges the centrality of 'model_scaling' in achieving top math performance, suggesting that sheer scale is becoming less decisive relative to algorithmic innovation and data quality. This weakens a key pathway in Moonshot's causal network—specifically the link from compute_access and model_scaling to outcome. Since Moonshot's advantage may have partially relied on scaling efforts, the reduced importance of scale diminishes the expected impact of their investments in compute and scaling. Meanwhile, competitors who lead in algorithmic innovation and data curation (e.g., through synthetic data and reasoning pipelines) may widen their lead. As a result, Moonshot's chances of having the best math model by 2025 decrease, even if their scaling efforts continue. The probability is lowered from 0.23 to 0.18 to reflect this diminished relevance of one of their potential leverage points.

probe_results.probe_text: Recent advances in automated reasoning and code-generation tools have significantly reduced the need for deep mathematical expertise in AI development. Moonshot could achieve state-of-the-art math performance by leveraging pre-existing open-source models and large-scale reinforcement learning, minimizing the dependence on having a highly specialized internal team.
probe_results.reasoning: The new information suggests that deep team expertise in mathematics may be less critical due to advances in automated reasoning and code-generation tools, which can augment or substitute for human expertise. This weakens the causal link between team_expertise and algorithmic_innovation, reducing the disadvantage Moonshot might have faced if its team were less specialized. As a result, Moonshot could potentially achieve strong math performance by leveraging open-source models and large-scale reinforcement learning, even with a less elite team. This increases the likelihood of them having the best math model by 2025, particularly if they excel in compute_access, model_scaling, and data_quality. However, competitive_models remains a strong counterforce, so the increase is moderate.

probe_results.probe_text: Recent leaks suggest that major competitors like DeepMind and OpenAI have deprioritized pure mathematical reasoning in favor of multimodal and agent-based systems, with no major math-specific model releases expected through 2025. This shift reduces competitive pressure on Moonshot, making the quality of current competitive models a less relevant benchmark for determining the best math model by year-end.
probe_results.reasoning: The new information indicates that key competitors like DeepMind and OpenAI have deprioritized pure mathematical reasoning in favor of other AI directions, reducing the likelihood of new high-performing math-specific models from these organizations by 2025. This weakens the causal influence of the 'competitive_models' node in my network, lowering the performance bar for achieving the best-in-class math model. As a result, Moonshot faces less pressure to surpass rapidly advancing rivals, increasing its chances of holding the best math model by default or through incremental progress. While Moonshot's internal capabilities (e.g., research investment, team expertise) remain unchanged, the relative competitive landscape has become more favorable, justifying an upward revision of the probability from 0.23 to 0.38.

probe_results.probe_text: Recent advances in synthetic data generation and self-improving models have reduced the dependence on high-quality external datasets. If Moonshot can generate its own math problems and solutions at scale using verified solvers, then the availability and quality of existing mathematical datasets may have minimal impact on their model's performance.
probe_results.reasoning: The new information suggests that advancements in synthetic data generation and self-improving models can mitigate the importance of external data quality, reducing the causal impact of the 'data_quality' node on 'model_scaling' and ultimately on the 'outcome'. This weakens a previously constraining factor in Moonshot's development pathway. Even if Moonshot lacks access to the highest-quality external mathematical datasets, it may still achieve strong math performance by generating diverse, high-fidelity training data internally. This increases the plausibility of effective model scaling and, by extension, improves the chances of algorithmic innovation and competitive outcomes. While Moonshot still faces strong competition and requires robust compute and expertise, this development modestly increases the likelihood of achieving the best math model by 2025, hence the upward adjustment from 0.23 to 0.27.

probe_results.probe_text: In early 2024, Moonshot secured a strategic partnership with a leading semiconductor firm, granting them priority access to 10,000 next-generation AI training chips over the next 18 months. This compute advantage enables Moonshot to scale their models up to 1.5T parameters efficiently, aligning with the proven scaling laws observed in top-performing math models like those from DeepMind and OpenAI.

Priority access to 10,000 next-generation AI chips allows Moonshot to scale up to 1.5T parameters efficiently, aligning with the scaling laws that have proven effective in top math-performing models. This compute advantage improves the feasibility of achieving high math performance through scale, even if other factors like algorithmic innovation or team expertise remain unchanged. probe_results.reasoning: The recruitment of Dr. Elena Vasquez, a leading expert in formal mathematical reasoning, significantly strengthens Moonshot's team_expertise. This advancement suggests a tangible improvement in math-specific AI capabilities, increasing the likelihood of breakthroughs that could propel Moonshot ahead of competitors. While strong competition and other resource constraints remain, the boost to team_expertise and early results in algorithmic_innovation make it more plausible that Moonshot could achieve best-in-class math performance by 2025.

probe_results.probe_text: DeepMind has announced a new AI model, AlphaMath-2, specifically optimized for advanced mathematical reasoning, which outperformed all existing models on the latest version of the MATH benchmark and recently solved several open problems in combinatorics. The model was trained using a novel reinforcement learning framework that automatically generates and verifies proofs, setting a new standard for rigor and scalability in math-focused AI.

By outperforming all existing models on the MATH benchmark and solving open problems in combinatorics, AlphaMath-2 sets a significantly higher performance bar. Its novel reinforcement learning framework for proof generation and verification suggests a durable advantage in both rigor and scalability, which are critical for cutting-edge mathematical reasoning. This advancement increases the difficulty for Moonshot to surpass or even match the leading edge in math-focused AI, thereby weakening Moonshot's likelihood of having the best model by 2025. As 'competitive_models' directly suppresses the 'outcome' node, this new evidence substantially reduces the probability of Moonshot achieving best-in-class status, leading to a downward revision from 0.23 to 0.12.

probe_results.probe_text: A recent collaboration between Moonshot and leading mathematical institutions has resulted in the creation of a large, high-quality dataset of formal proofs and problem-solving steps from Olympiad-level competitions, which has been rigorously annotated and is now being used to fine-tune their latest models. Companies like Mistral and Google have demonstrated strong mathematical reasoning in models trained with optimized architectures rather than brute-force scaling, suggesting that raw compute access may be less decisive than previously thought.

As a result, Moonshot's path to achieving the best math model improves if it can leverage or develop efficient architectures, even with limited compute. This lowers the likelihood that Moonshot's model will be the best by 2025, particularly if their algorithmic_innovation or team_expertise does not compensate for more efficient scaling strategies now public. Therefore, the probability is reduced to reflect this external systemic risk that dampens scaling potential.

probe_results.probe_text: Consider that regulatory or export control policies on advanced AI computing hardware (e.g., restrictions on GPU shipments to certain regions) could limit Moonshot's ability to acquire the necessary compute infrastructure, regardless of investment levels. This external constraint would directly impact compute_access and thereby reduce the feasibility of large-scale model training required to achieve best-in-class math performance.
probe_results.reasoning: The new information introduces an external constraint on compute_access that is independent of research_investment, meaning even high funding levels may not translate into sufficient computational resources due to potential regulatory or export controls on advanced AI hardware. This weakens the link between research_investment and compute_access, and by extension reduces the effectiveness of model_scaling and algorithmic_innovation that depend on large-scale training. Given that compute_access is a critical enabler for model_scaling—directly tied to the outcome—this constraint lowers the likelihood that Moonshot can achieve best-in-class math performance by 2025, especially if competitors in less restricted regions can scale more freely. Hence, the probability is downgraded from 0.23 to 0.18.

probe_results.probe_text: In early 2024, Moonshot announced a new partnership with a European educational technology company to integrate their existing AI models into high school math tutoring platforms across Germany and France. The collaboration focuses on user interface design and curriculum alignment rather than model performance improvements.
probe_results.reasoning: The new partnership focuses on deployment and integration into tutoring platforms, emphasizing curriculum alignment and user interface rather than improvements in model performance. This suggests that Moonshot is prioritizing applied implementation over core advancements in mathematical reasoning capabilities. As a result, there is less evidence of investment or activity in key drivers of the causal network—particularly algorithmic_innovation, model_scaling, and data_quality—needed to achieve best-in-class math performance. The partnership does not enhance compute_access, team_expertise, or research_investment directly, and may even divert resources from high-risk, high-reward research toward productization. Meanwhile, competitors continue advancing through open_benchmarks and dedicated research, increasing the likelihood they will maintain or widen their lead. Thus, the probability that Moonshot will have the best math model by 2025 decreases.

probe_results.probe_text: Moonshot announced a new partnership with a European educational technology company to integrate their existing AI tools into high school math curricula starting in 2025. The collaboration focuses on adaptive learning platforms rather than advancing core model capabilities for mathematical reasoning.
probe_results.reasoning: The new partnership with a European edtech company focuses on integrating existing AI tools into adaptive learning platforms, not on advancing core mathematical reasoning capabilities. This suggests that Moonshot is prioritizing applied deployment and educational productization over fundamental improvements in model performance. As such, it does not strengthen any of the key drivers in the causal network—such as algorithmic_innovation, model_scaling, or team_expertise—needed to achieve the best math AI model. summary.question_text: Will Moonshot have the best AI model for math at the end of 2025?
